homework.title: Identifying Chemical Properties
homework.instructions: Select the correct option for each question based on the properties of chemical substances discussed in class.

question.q_text: Which of the following is a physical property?
Which substance turns blue litmus paper red?
The reaction of a metal with oxygen to form a metal oxide is an example of:
Which property allows a substance to be drawn into wires?

homework.title: Differentiating Physical and Chemical Changes
homework.instructions: Define physical and chemical changes. Provide two examples for each from your daily life.
homework.question: Explain the difference between a physical change and a chemical change with examples.
homework.answerSpace: Write your answer in 3-4 sentences per example.
homework.expectedResponse: Physical change: No new substance is formed (e.g., melting ice). Chemical change: New substances are formed with different properties (e.g., rusting iron).

homework.title: Observation to Conclusion
homework.instructions: Draw a labelled diagram of the reaction between magnesium ribbon and oxygen. Describe the observation and write the balanced chemical equation.
homework.question: Describe the experiment where magnesium burns in air. Draw the setup and write the equation.
homework.answerSpace: Include a sketch of the burning magnesium and the resulting ash.
homework.visualRequirement: Draw a labelled diagram showing the burning magnesium ribbon, the flame, and the white ash (Magnesium Oxide).
homework.expectedResponse: Observation: Bright white light and white powder. Equation: 2Mg + O2 -> 2MgO

homework.title: Corrosion and Prevention
homework.instructions: Identify a common chemical property of iron that causes damage to vehicles. Suggest two methods to prevent it.
homework.question: Why do iron gates rust? Explain the chemical process and suggest two ways to stop it.
homework.answerSpace: Write a short paragraph explaining the cause and prevention.
homework.expectedResponse: Cause: Reaction with oxygen and moisture (oxidation). Prevention: Painting, greasing, galvanization.

homework.title: Observing Chemical Changes at Home
homework.instructions: Perform a safe home activity to observe a chemical change. Record your observations.
homework.question: Mix vinegar and baking soda in a small bowl. Observe the reaction and write down what you see.
homework.answerSpace: Record the bubbles, sound, or temperature change observed.
homework.expectedResponse: Bubbles of gas (CO2) are released, indicating a chemical change.
